case_name: GWH v Zog Limited [2022] NZERA 536 (17 October 2022)

summary.brief: GWH v Zog Limited [2022] NZERA 536 (17 October 2022). Medical malpractice case involving psychiatry.

evidence_index.text: GWH had furnished a medical certificate covering her absence up to 29 January 2021.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: ZOG Ltd had not concluded agreement and signalled they now wanted to review GWH’s ongoing employment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: GWH identified a personal grievance of disadvantage and constructive dismissal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: GWH was not constructively dismissed but was the subject of an unjustified action causing her to suffer a disadvantage.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: ZOG Limited must pay GWH the sum of $5,000 compensation without deductions.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I find ZOG Ltd carefully considered GWH’s medical information and sought regular updates.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Fair process includes: prior notice of an intention to dismiss, exposition of the reasoning for such and, input from the employee.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Absence from work due to medical condition
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: GWH had furnished a medical certificate covering her absence up to 29 January 2021. She indicated a good prognosis following surgery with up to six weeks recovery time. However, no surgery date was provided.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Medical leave due to unspecified condition
critical_findings: Absence from work due to medical condition

requestables.name: Medical Certificate
requestables.description: Certificate covering absence up to 29 January 2021

timeline_phases.name: Initial Medical Leave
timeline_phases.description: GWH provides medical certificate for absence

decision_points.prompt: What should the employer do regarding GWH's employment status?

options.description: Continue to hold GWH's position open until surgery date is confirmed
options.clinical_reasoning: Allows for fair process and consideration of medical condition

options.description: Proceed with reviewing GWH's employment status due to prolonged absence
options.clinical_reasoning: Based on business needs and uncertainty of return

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with reviewing employment status
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Due to prolonged absence and business impact

expected_action_court.description: Position should have been held open until surgery date confirmed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Fair process and consideration of medical condition

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on evidence E006 and E079
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Position should have been held open

patient_outcome.description: GWH faced employment challenges due to medical leave

key_findings: GWH was not constructively dismissed but was subject to unjustified action

court_findings.finding: GWH was not constructively dismissed but was subject to unjustified action
